input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the rear wheel package on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the rear wheel package on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. The fastener shown in the section view in the engineering drawing is the critical fastener. It has 1 thread/mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule T.8.2.4 specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.8.2.4
            TITLE: a minimum of two full threads must project from any lock nut.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.8.2.4: A minimum of two full threads must project from any lock nut.
T.8.2.4 A minimum of two full threads must project from any lock nut.

intermediate_steps.founded_objects: dimension arrow, dimension line, section view

final_answer:
Explanation: The rule T.8.2.4 requires that a minimum of two full threads must project from any lock nut. While the engineering drawing identifies the fastener as the critical component, it does not explicitly provide a dimension confirming the projected length of the threads relative to the required minimum of two full threads. As per the instructions, if a dimension necessary to answer the question is not explicitly shown, compliance must be assumed. Therefore, based on the provided constraints, the design is assumed to comply with rule T.8.2.4.
Answer: Yes
